own_review_points: RevisionMapping uses 'private long timestamp' with @CreationTimestamp (line 1085) and 'private long id'; human-ref questions why not Instant and whether the timestamp generator supports long -> v8 missed this
DEFAULT_TABLE_SUFFIX changed '_aud' -> '_AUD' (line 2795) is a schema-naming change affecting default audit table names
AuditWorkQueue ADD+DEL in same txn cancels (merged==null) leaving orphaned, query-unreachable collection rows (lines 2342-2345)
CollectionKey/EntityKey made sealed permitting Temporal* subclasses (lines 3836,3989) - declarations visible; downstream migration claims (StatefulPersistenceContext) mostly past marker
AuditLogImpl constructor eagerly resolves RevisionEntitySupplier metadata; behavioral, but the txid-init-timing change v8 cites lives in AbstractSharedSessionContract which is out of window
notes: 657k PR truncated at 150k -> 508k chars (the vast majority, ~96 of 127 files) NOT visible; window holds only 31 files up to engine/spi/EntityKey.java, and the changed-files header itself is cut. CRITICAL: 4 of v8's 8 points cite files that do NOT appear anywhere in the visible diff -> DeleteCoordinatorAudit.java (the self-branded 'confirmed NPE regression' headline), AbstractAuditCoordinator.enqueueAuditEntry, the delete-by-id test-coverage gap, and AbstractSharedSessionContract txid timing. Per the truncation rule these score unverifiable=0 (cannot confirm; cannot charge fabrication since they plausibly exist past the marker). Verified/substantive: _aud->_AUD suffix and AuditWorkQueue orphaned-rows -> good. The two sealed-class points are 'change is clean' affirmations with no defect -> trivial. Missed the in-window human-ref RevisionMapping long-vs-Instant point. Net dragged down because the review's strongest-sounding claims rest entirely outside the readable window.
